---
title: "Plasma_DEP_anlaysis"
author: "Savanah Leidholt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
.libPaths("~/leidholt/R")
```


```{r}
# ---- packages ----
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(limma)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(vegan)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(purrr)
library(haven)
```

#---------------------------------------------------
# inputting all directories and storage locations
#---------------------------------------------------
```{r}
#Directories
#computer <- "mac studio"
computer <- "mac laptop"
if (computer == "mac studio") {
  user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]
  if (user == "sleidholt") {
    dir.dat <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/sleidholt/Documents/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad/PANTHER PFAS/"
  }
} else {
  user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]
  if (user == "savanahleidholt") {
    dir.dat <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/savanahleidholt/Desktop/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
  } 
}
```


```{r}
# ---- load split objects created in your first Rmd ----
obj <- readRDS("/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/R_objects/omics_split_objects3.rds")

meta   <- obj$meta_prot
prot_plasma <- obj$prot_plasma
n_table     <- obj$n_table

# quick check
n_table
dim(prot_plasma)
dim(meta)


```
```{r}
#--------------------------
#open panther_data.RData
#--------------------------

load("/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/R_objects/panther_data.RData")


#trasnforming data to match the harmonized data
panther_hba1c <- panther %>%
  transmute(
    record_id,
    hba1c_panther = as.numeric(zap_labels(hba1c))
  )
```

```{r}
panther_hba1c$record_id <- as.character(panther_hba1c$record_id)

# 2) Make sure harm$hba1c is numeric too (if it exists)
meta <- meta %>%
  mutate(hba1c = as.numeric(zap_labels(hba1c))) %>%
  left_join(panther_hba1c, by = "record_id") %>%
  mutate(
    hba1c = if_else(
      study == "PANTHER" & is.na(hba1c),
      hba1c_panther,
      hba1c
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-hba1c_panther)
```


```{r}
# ---- clean + filter meta ----
meta$record_id <- trimws(as.character(meta$record_id))
meta$study     <- trimws(as.character(meta$study))
meta$group     <- trimws(as.character(meta$group))
meta$sex       <- trimws(as.character(meta$sex))
meta$ethnicity <- trimws(as.character(meta$race_ethnicity_condensed))

# ensure numeric age (prevents silent coercion issues)
meta$age <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(meta$age))

# drop missing covariates (common set for PCA + limma)
keep <- !is.na(meta$age) & !is.na(meta$sex) & meta$sex != "" 
meta <- meta[keep, , drop = FALSE]

# compact group labels for plots/models
meta$group4 <- factor(
  meta$group,
  levels = c("Lean Control","Obese Control","Type 1 Diabetes","Type 2 Diabetes"),
  labels = c("LC","OC","T1D","T2D")
)

# ethnicity as factor (from cleaned column)
meta$ethnicity <- factor(meta$ethnicity)

# study6: SAFE mapping (never creates NA; unlisted studies keep original name)
meta$study6 <- dplyr::recode(
  meta$study,
  "ATTEMPT"        = "ATT",
  "CROCODILE"      = "CRC",
  "PANTHER"        = "PAN",
  "RENAL-HEIR"     = "RH",
  "IMPROVE"        = "IT",
  "RENAL-HEIRitage"= "RH2",
  .default = meta$study
)
meta$study6 <- factor(meta$study6)

# meta_common is your analysis baseline set (age+sex present)
meta_common <- meta
meta_common <- meta_common %>%
  mutate(
    uacr = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(acr_u)),
    log_uacr = if_else(is.na(uacr), NA_real_, log(uacr + 1))
  )

```

```{r}
# Make both comparable
meta_common$record_id <- trimws(as.character(meta_common$record_id))
prot_ids <- trimws(rownames(prot_plasma))

cat("meta_common n:", nrow(meta_common), "\n")
cat("prot_plasma dim:", dim(prot_plasma), "\n")

cat("example meta IDs:", paste(head(meta_common$record_id), collapse=", "), "\n")
cat("example prot rownames:", paste(head(prot_ids), collapse=", "), "\n")

# overlap
olap <- intersect(meta_common$record_id, prot_ids)
cat("overlap n:", length(olap), "\n")

# show a few that DON'T match (helps spot formatting differences)
cat("meta not in prot:", sum(!meta_common$record_id %in% prot_ids), "\n")
cat("prot not in meta:", sum(!prot_ids %in% meta_common$record_id), "\n")
```


```{r}
# ---- align to matrix IDs ----
meta_common$record_id <- trimws(as.character(meta_common$record_id))
rownames(prot_plasma) <- trimws(rownames(prot_plasma))

olap <- intersect(meta_common$record_id, rownames(prot_plasma))
cat("Overlap samples (meta vs plasma):", length(olap), "\n")
stopifnot(length(olap) > 0)

meta_common <- meta_common[match(olap, meta_common$record_id), , drop = FALSE]
prot_plasma_common <- prot_plasma[olap, , drop = FALSE]

stopifnot(identical(rownames(prot_plasma_common), meta_common$record_id))

table(meta_common$study, meta_common$group4, useNA = "ifany")
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# PCA — Visual adjustment matching limma models M1, M2, M3, M4
# Colored by group4, shaped by study.
# =============================================================================

# ---- use all samples with age + sex present (meta_common baseline) ----
meta_pca <- meta_common
prot_pca  <- prot_plasma_common[meta_pca$record_id, , drop = FALSE]
stopifnot(identical(rownames(prot_pca), meta_pca$record_id))
prot_pca <- as.matrix(prot_pca)

# ---- log2 transform + median impute ----
prot_log <- log2(prot_pca + 1)
prot_log[!is.finite(prot_log)] <- NA_real_

prot_imp <- prot_log
meds <- apply(prot_imp, 2, function(x) {
  m <- median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.finite(m)) m else NA_real_
})
for (j in seq_len(ncol(prot_imp))) {
  if (!is.na(meds[j])) prot_imp[is.na(prot_imp[, j]), j] <- meds[j]
}

# drop zero-variance / still-NA columns
keep_cols <- colSums(is.na(prot_imp)) == 0 &
             apply(prot_imp, 2, var) > 0 &
             is.finite(apply(prot_imp, 2, var))
prot_imp  <- prot_imp[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]
cat("Proteins kept for PCA:", ncol(prot_imp), "\n")

# ---- shared plot helper ----
# builds a consistent ggplot from a PCA result + metadata
make_pca_plot <- function(prot_mat, meta_df, title_str) {
  pca  <- prcomp(prot_mat, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  vexp <- round(100 * (pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2), 1)

  tibble(
    PC1    = pca$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca$x[, 2],
    group4 = meta_df$group4,
    study  = meta_df$study6
  ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(PC1, PC2, color = group4, shape = study)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(LC  = "#2166AC",
                 OC  = "#F4A582",
                 T1D = "#D6604D",
                 T2D = "#4DAC26"),
      name = "Group"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_str,
      x     = paste0("PC1 (", vexp[1], "%)"),
      y     = paste0("PC2 (", vexp[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
}

# =============================================================================
# PCA 1 — No adjustment  (all samples)
# =============================================================================
p <- make_pca_plot(prot_imp, meta_pca, "No adjustment")

# =============================================================================
# PCA 2 — Age + Sex  (M1 equivalent, all samples)
# =============================================================================
cov_m1 <- model.matrix(~ age + sex, data = meta_pca)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
prot_m1 <- t(removeBatchEffect(t(prot_imp), covariates = cov_m1))

p1 <- make_pca_plot(prot_m1, meta_pca, "Age + Sex")

# =============================================================================
# PCA 3 — Age + Sex + BMI  (M5, drops missing BMI)
# =============================================================================
keep_m2   <- !is.na(meta_pca$bmi)
meta_m2p  <- meta_pca[keep_m2, ]
prot_m2p  <- prot_imp[keep_m2, , drop = FALSE]

cov_m2 <- model.matrix(~ age + sex + bmi, data = meta_m2p)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
prot_m2_adj <- t(removeBatchEffect(t(prot_m2p), covariates = cov_m2))

p2 <- make_pca_plot(prot_m2_adj, meta_m2p, "Age + Sex + BMI")

# =============================================================================
# PCA 3 — Age + Sex + BMI + HbA1c  (M3, drops missing BMI or HbA1c)
# =============================================================================
keep_m3   <- !is.na(meta_pca$bmi) & !is.na(meta_pca$hba1c)
meta_m3p  <- meta_pca[keep_m3, ]
prot_m3p  <- prot_imp[keep_m3, , drop = FALSE]

cov_m3 <- model.matrix(~ age + sex + bmi + hba1c, data = meta_m3p)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
prot_m3_adj <- t(removeBatchEffect(t(prot_m3p), covariates = cov_m3))

p3 <- make_pca_plot(prot_m3_adj, meta_m3p, "Age + Sex + BMI + HbA1c")

# =============================================================================
# PCA 4 — Age + Sex + BMI + HbA1c + log(uACR)  (M4, fully adjusted)
# =============================================================================

keep_m4   <- !is.na(meta_pca$bmi) & !is.na(meta_pca$hba1c) & !is.na(meta_pca$log_uacr)
meta_m4p  <- meta_pca[keep_m4, ]
prot_m4p  <- prot_imp[keep_m4, , drop = FALSE]

cov_m4 <- model.matrix(~ age + sex + bmi + hba1c + log_uacr,
                        data = meta_m4p)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
prot_m4_adj <- t(removeBatchEffect(t(prot_m4p), covariates = cov_m4))

p4 <- make_pca_plot(prot_m4_adj, meta_m4p, "Age + Sex + BMI + HbA1c + log(uACR)")

# =============================================================================
# RENDER — 2 rows: unadjusted on top, progressively adjusted below
# =============================================================================
(p | p1 | p2) /
(p3 | p4 | plot_spacer()) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "PCA: Progressive Covariate Adjustment",
    subtitle = "Color = group  |  Shape = study  |  Variance removed via removeBatchEffect (visual only)",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9,  hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40")
    )
  )


```




## limma sensitivity testing
```{r}
## limma sensitivity testing (plasma)
# Consistent factors across all models
meta_common$sex    <- factor(meta_common$sex)
meta_common$group4 <- factor(meta_common$group4)

# helper: run limma + return list of topTables
run_limma_contrasts <- function(Y, design, contr) {
  fit <- lmFit(Y, design)
  fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contr)
  fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)

  out <- setNames(vector("list", ncol(contr)), colnames(contr))
  for (cc in colnames(contr)) {
    tt <- topTable(fit2, coef = cc, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH", sort.by = "P")
    tt$feature_id <- rownames(tt)
    tt$contrast <- cc
    out[[cc]] <- tt
  }
  list(fit = fit2, out = out)
}

# response matrix (proteins x samples)
Y_all <- t(log2(as.matrix(prot_plasma_common) + 1))
```


```{r}
# ===================
# MODEL 1: age + sex
# ===================
meta_m1 <- meta_common
Y_m1 <- Y_all[, meta_m1$record_id, drop = FALSE]  # enforce same sample order

design_no <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group4 + age + sex, data = meta_m1)
colnames(design_no) <- sub("^group4", "", colnames(design_no))
colnames(design_no) <- make.names(colnames(design_no))

contr_m1 <- makeContrasts(
  T1D_vs_LC   = T1D - LC,
  T2D_vs_LC   = T2D - LC,
  T1D_vs_T2D  = T1D - T2D,
  OC_vs_LC    = OC  - LC,
  T1D_vs_OC   = T1D - OC,
  T2D_vs_OC   = T2D - OC,
  levels = design_no
)

res_m1 <- run_limma_contrasts(Y_m1, design_no, contr_m1)
fit_m1 <- res_m1$fit
out_m1  <- res_m1$out

sig_m1 <- tibble(
  contrast = colnames(contr_m1),
  n_FDR05 = sapply(out_m1, \(tt) sum(tt$adj.P.Val < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)),
  n_FDR01 = sapply(out_m1, \(tt) sum(tt$adj.P.Val < 0.01, na.rm = TRUE))
) %>% mutate(model = "age + sex", n_samples = nrow(meta_m1))

sig_m1
```




```{r}
# =============================================================================
# LIMMA SENSITIVITY TESTING — EXTENDED MODELS
# Adds: BMI, HbA1c, uACR 
# Assumes: run_limma_contrasts(), Y_all, meta_common already exist from
#          your existing Rmd
# =============================================================================

# ---- ensure covariates are numeric ----
meta_common$bmi   <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(meta_common$bmi))
meta_common$hba1c <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(meta_common$hba1c))
meta_common$uacr  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(meta_common$acr_u))

# NOTE: uACR is typically right-skewed — log-transform before entering model
# log(uACR + 1) to handle zeros if present
meta_common$log_uacr <- log(meta_common$uacr + 1)

# ---- helper: summarise sig hits per contrast ----
summarise_sig <- function(out_list, model_label, n_samp) {
  tibble(
    contrast = names(out_list),
    n_FDR05  = sapply(out_list, \(tt) sum(tt$adj.P.Val < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)),
    n_FDR01  = sapply(out_list, \(tt) sum(tt$adj.P.Val < 0.01, na.rm = TRUE)),
    model    = model_label,
    n_samples = n_samp
  )
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# MODEL 2: age + sex + BMI
# =============================================================================
meta_m2 <- meta_common %>%
  filter(!is.na(bmi))           # drop missing BMI only

Y_m2 <- Y_all[, meta_m2$record_id, drop = FALSE]

design_m2 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group4 + age + sex + bmi, data = meta_m2)
colnames(design_m2) <- sub("^group4", "", colnames(design_m2))
colnames(design_m2) <- make.names(colnames(design_m2))

contr_m2 <- makeContrasts(
  T1D_vs_LC  = T1D - LC,
  T2D_vs_LC  = T2D - LC,
  T1D_vs_T2D = T1D - T2D,
  OC_vs_LC   = OC  - LC,
  T1D_vs_OC  = T1D - OC,
  T2D_vs_OC  = T2D - OC,
  levels = design_m2
)

res_m2  <- run_limma_contrasts(Y_m2, design_m2, contr_m2)
out_m2  <- res_m2$out
fit_m2  <- res_m2$fit
sig_m2  <- summarise_sig(out_m2, "age_sex_bmi", nrow(meta_m2))

cat("Model 2 (age+sex+BMI) complete. N =", nrow(meta_m2), "\n")
sig_m2
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# MODEL 3: age + sex + BMI + HbA1c
# =============================================================================
meta_m3 <- meta_common %>%
  filter(!is.na(bmi), !is.na(hba1c))

Y_m3 <- Y_all[, meta_m3$record_id, drop = FALSE]

design_m3 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group4 + age + sex + bmi + hba1c, data = meta_m3)
colnames(design_m3) <- sub("^group4", "", colnames(design_m3))
colnames(design_m3) <- make.names(colnames(design_m3))

contr_m3 <- makeContrasts(
  T1D_vs_LC  = T1D - LC,
  T2D_vs_LC  = T2D - LC,
  T1D_vs_T2D = T1D - T2D,
  OC_vs_LC   = OC  - LC,
  T1D_vs_OC  = T1D - OC,
  T2D_vs_OC  = T2D - OC,
  levels = design_m3
)

res_m3  <- run_limma_contrasts(Y_m3, design_m3, contr_m3)
out_m3  <- res_m3$out
fit_m3  <- res_m3$fit
sig_m3  <- summarise_sig(out_m3, "age_sex_bmi_hba1c", nrow(meta_m3))

cat("Model 3 (age+sex+BMI+HbA1c) complete. N =", nrow(meta_m3), "\n")
sig_m3
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# MODEL 4: age + sex + BMI + HbA1c + log(uACR)
# =============================================================================
meta_m4 <- meta_common %>%
  filter(!is.na(bmi), !is.na(hba1c), !is.na(log_uacr))

Y_m4 <- Y_all[, meta_m4$record_id, drop = FALSE]

design_m4 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group4 + age + sex + bmi + hba1c + log_uacr,
                           data = meta_m4)
colnames(design_m4) <- sub("^group4", "", colnames(design_m4))
colnames(design_m4) <- make.names(colnames(design_m4))

contr_m4 <- makeContrasts(
  T1D_vs_LC  = T1D - LC,
  T2D_vs_LC  = T2D - LC,
  T1D_vs_T2D = T1D - T2D,
  OC_vs_LC   = OC  - LC,
  T1D_vs_OC  = T1D - OC,
  T2D_vs_OC  = T2D - OC,
  levels = design_m4
)

res_m4  <- run_limma_contrasts(Y_m4, design_m4, contr_m4)
out_m4  <- res_m4$out
fit_m4  <- res_m4$fit
sig_m4  <- summarise_sig(out_m4, "age_sex_bmi_hba1c_uacr", nrow(meta_m4))

cat("Model 4 (age+sex+BMI+HbA1c+log_uACR) complete. N =", nrow(meta_m4), "\n")
sig_m4
```



```{r}
# =============================================================================
# COMBINED SUMMARY TABLE (all models including your originals)
# =============================================================================
# Assumes sig_no and sig_with already exist from your original code
summary_all <- bind_rows(
  sig_m1  %>% mutate(model = "age_sex"),
  sig_m2,
  sig_m3,
  sig_m4
) %>%
  select(model, n_samples, contrast, n_FDR05, n_FDR01) %>%
  arrange(contrast, model)

print(summary_all)

# Optional: wide format for easy comparison across models per contrast
summary_wide <- summary_all %>%
  select(model, contrast, n_FDR05) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = n_FDR05)

print(summary_wide)
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# COLLINEARITY CHECK — VIF (variance inflation factors)
# Run this to flag problematic collinearity before trusting model results
# =============================================================================

library(car)

# Use model 4 design (most covariates) as the stress test
# Build a standard (non-zero-intercept) model for VIF calculation
vif_meta <- meta_m4
vif_formula <- lm(rep(0, nrow(vif_meta)) ~ group4 + age + sex + bmi + hba1c + log_uacr,
                  data = vif_meta)
vif_vals <- vif(vif_formula, type = "predictor")
print(vif_vals)
# Rule of thumb: VIF > 5 warrants concern; VIF > 10 is serious collinearity
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# SAVE EXTENDED MODEL OUTPUTS
# =============================================================================
saveRDS(
  list(
    #Model 1
    fit_m1 = fit_m1, out_m1 = out_m1,
    meta_m1 = meta_m1, design_m1 = design_no,
   
     # Model 2: + BMI
    fit_m2 = fit_m2, out_m2 = out_m2,
    meta_m2 = meta_m2, design_m2 = design_m2,

    # Model 3: + BMI + HbA1c
    fit_m3 = fit_m3, out_m3 = out_m3,
    meta_m3 = meta_m3, design_m3 = design_m3,

    # Model 7: + BMI + HbA1c + log_uACR
    fit_m4 = fit_m4, out_m4 = out_m4,
    meta_m4 = meta_m4, design_m4 = design_m4,

    # Summary tables
    summary_all  = summary_all,
    summary_wide = summary_wide
  ),
  file = "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/R_objects/limma_outputs_extended.rds"
)

cat("Extended models saved.\n")
```

```{r}
#making venn diagrams to check out overlaps in proteins
#can run independently of above code

# =============================================================================
# VENN DIAGRAMS: DEP Overlap Across Models per Contrast
# Models compared:
#   M1: age + sex only         (out_m1)
#   M2: + BMI                  (out_m5)
#   M3: + BMI + HbA1c          (out_m6)
#   M4: + BMI + HbA1c + uACR   (out_m7)
# =============================================================================

# ---- packages ----
library(ggVennDiagram)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# ---- FDR threshold ----
FDR_THRESH <- 0.05

# ---- contrasts to plot ----
contrasts <- c("T1D_vs_LC", "T2D_vs_LC", "T1D_vs_T2D",
               "OC_vs_LC",  "T1D_vs_OC", "T2D_vs_OC")

# ---- helper: extract significant feature IDs for a given model + contrast ----
get_sig <- function(out_list, contrast, fdr = FDR_THRESH) {
  tt <- out_list[[contrast]]
  if (is.null(tt)) return(character(0))
  tt %>%
    filter(!is.na(adj.P.Val), adj.P.Val < fdr) %>%
    pull(feature_id)
}
```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# OPTION A: One Venn per contrast (4-way: M1, M2, M3, M4)
# Produces a 2x3 grid
# =============================================================================

plots_4way <- lapply(contrasts, function(cc) {

  sets <- list(
    "Age+Sex"             = get_sig(out_m1, cc),
    "Age+Sex+BMI"         = get_sig(out_m2, cc),
    "Age+Sex+BMI+HbA1c"   = get_sig(out_m3, cc),
    "+uACR"               = get_sig(out_m4, cc)
  )

  # skip if all sets are empty
  if (all(sapply(sets, length) == 0)) {
    return(
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = paste0(cc, "\n(no sig. DEPs)"),
                 size = 4) +
        theme_void() +
        labs(title = cc)
    )
  }

  ggVennDiagram(
    sets,
    label       = "count",
    label_alpha = 0,
    set_size    = 3.2
  ) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F5F5F5", high = "#4575B4") +
    labs(title = cc) +
    theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
})

names(plots_4way) <- contrasts

# ---- render 2x3 grid ----
wrap_plots(plots_4way, ncol = 3) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "DEP Overlap Across Covariate Models (FDR < 0.05)",
    subtitle = "Each region shows proteins significant in that model combination only",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40")
    )
  )
```


```{r}

# =============================================================================
# OPTION B: Focused 2-way — "Core" DEPs stable across all 4 models
# Useful for identifying the most robust hits
# =============================================================================

core_stable <- lapply(contrasts, function(cc) {

  sig_m2 <- get_sig(out_m2, cc)
  sig_m4 <- get_sig(out_m4, cc)

  sets <- list(
    "(M2)"      = sig_m2,
    "(M4)"     = sig_m4
  )

  n_core <- length(intersect(sig_m2, sig_m4))
  n_lost <- length(setdiff(sig_m2, sig_m4))   # sig in M1 but lost after adjustment
  n_new  <- length(setdiff(sig_m4, sig_m2))   # newly sig after adjustment

  ggVennDiagram(
    sets,
    label       = "count",
    label_alpha = 0,
    set_size    = 3.5
  ) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#FFF5EB", high = "#D94801") +
    labs(
      title    = cc,
      subtitle = paste0("Core: ", n_core,
                        " | Lost after adj.: ", n_lost,
                        " | New after adj.: ", n_new)
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8,  color = "grey40"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
})

names(core_stable) <- contrasts

wrap_plots(core_stable, ncol = 3) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Core DEPs: Age+Sex+BMI Model vs. Fully Adjusted Model (FDR < 0.05)",
    subtitle = "'Lost' = attenuated by BMI/HbA1c/uACR  |  'New' = unmasked after adjustment",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40")
    )
  )

```


```{r}
# =============================================================================
# BONUS: Print core stable protein lists per contrast (for downstream use)
# =============================================================================
cat("\n=== Core Stable DEPs (significant in BOTH M2 and M4) ===\n")
core_lists <- lapply(contrasts, function(cc) {
  core <- intersect(get_sig(out_m2, cc), get_sig(out_m4, cc))
  cat(cc, ":", length(core), "proteins\n")
  core
})
names(core_lists) <- contrasts

# Save core lists
#saveRDS(core_lists,
 #       "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/R_objects/core_stable_DEPs.rds")

```



```{r}
# =============================================================================
# EXTRA PCA — Age + Sex + Ethnicity (visual adjustment only)
# =============================================================================

# keep complete cases for these covariates
keep_eth_pca <- !is.na(meta_common$age) & !is.na(meta_common$sex) & !is.na(meta_common$ethnicity)
meta_eth_pca <- meta_common[keep_eth_pca, , drop = FALSE]

prot_eth_pca <- prot_plasma_common[meta_eth_pca$record_id, , drop = FALSE]
prot_eth_pca <- as.matrix(prot_eth_pca)

# log2 transform + median impute (same approach as above)
prot_log_e <- log2(prot_eth_pca + 1)
prot_log_e[!is.finite(prot_log_e)] <- NA_real_

prot_imp_e <- prot_log_e
meds_e <- apply(prot_imp_e, 2, function(x) {
  m <- median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.finite(m)) m else NA_real_
})
for (j in seq_len(ncol(prot_imp_e))) {
  if (!is.na(meds_e[j])) prot_imp_e[is.na(prot_imp_e[, j]), j] <- meds_e[j]
}

keep_cols_e <- colSums(is.na(prot_imp_e)) == 0 &
  apply(prot_imp_e, 2, var) > 0 &
  is.finite(apply(prot_imp_e, 2, var))
prot_imp_e <- prot_imp_e[, keep_cols_e, drop = FALSE]
cat("Proteins kept for PCA (age+sex+ethnicity):", ncol(prot_imp_e), "\n")

# ensure factors
meta_eth_pca$sex <- factor(meta_eth_pca$sex)
meta_eth_pca$ethnicity <- droplevels(factor(meta_eth_pca$ethnicity))

# removeBatchEffect covariates matrix (no intercept)
cov_eth <- model.matrix(~ age + sex + ethnicity, data = meta_eth_pca)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
prot_eth_adj <- t(removeBatchEffect(t(prot_imp_e), covariates = cov_eth))

# make PCA plot (reuse your helper)
p_eth <- make_pca_plot(prot_eth_adj, meta_eth_pca, "Age + Sex + Ethnicity")

p_eth

```


```{r}

### 2) limma: Model 5 = age + sex + ethnicity
# =============================================================================
# EXTRA LIMMA MODEL: age + sex + ethnicity
# =============================================================================

# ensure factors
meta_common$sex <- factor(meta_common$sex)
meta_common$group4 <- factor(meta_common$group4)
meta_common$ethnicity <- droplevels(factor(meta_common$ethnicity))

# keep complete cases for ethnicity model
meta_m5 <- meta_common %>%
  filter(!is.na(ethnicity))

Y_m5 <- Y_all[, meta_m5$record_id, drop = FALSE]

design_m5 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group4 + age + sex + ethnicity, data = meta_m5)
colnames(design_m5) <- sub("^group4", "", colnames(design_m5))
colnames(design_m5) <- make.names(colnames(design_m5))

contr_m5 <- makeContrasts(
  T1D_vs_LC  = T1D - LC,
  T2D_vs_LC  = T2D - LC,
  T1D_vs_T2D = T1D - T2D,
  OC_vs_LC   = OC  - LC,
  T1D_vs_OC  = T1D - OC,
  T2D_vs_OC  = T2D - OC,
  levels = design_m5
)

res_m5 <- run_limma_contrasts(Y_m5, design_m5, contr_m5)
fit_m5 <- res_m5$fit
out_m5 <- res_m5$out

sig_m5 <- summarise_sig(out_m5, "age_sex_ethnicity", nrow(meta_m5))

cat("Model 5 (age+sex+ethnicity) complete. N =", nrow(meta_m5), "\n")
sig_m5
```


```{r}



### 3) Add Model 5 into your combined summary table
# =============================================================================
# UPDATE SUMMARY TABLE TO INCLUDE ETHNICITY MODEL
# =============================================================================
summary_all <- bind_rows(
  sig_m1 %>% mutate(model = "age_sex"),
  sig_m2,
  sig_m3,
  sig_m4,
  sig_m5
) %>%
  select(model, n_samples, contrast, n_FDR05, n_FDR01) %>%
  arrange(contrast, model)

print(summary_all)

summary_wide <- summary_all %>%
  select(model, contrast, n_FDR05) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = n_FDR05)

print(summary_wide)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# RENDER — 2 rows: unadjusted on top, progressively adjusted below
# =============================================================================
(p | p1 | p2) /
(p3 | p4 | p_eth) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "PCA: Progressive Covariate Adjustment",
    subtitle = "Color = group  |  Shape = study  |  Variance removed via removeBatchEffect (visual only)",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9,  hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40")
    )
  )


```
```{r}

# =============================================================================
# OPTION A: One Venn per contrast (4-way: M1, M2, M3, M4)
# Produces a 2x3 grid
# =============================================================================

plots_4way <- lapply(contrasts, function(cc) {

  sets <- list(
    "Age+Sex"             = get_sig(out_m1, cc),
    "+BMI"         = get_sig(out_m2, cc),
    "+BMI+HbA1c"   = get_sig(out_m3, cc),
    "+BMI+HbA1c+uACR"               = get_sig(out_m4, cc),
    "Age+Sex+Ethnicity" =get_sig(out_m5, cc)
  ) 

  # skip if all sets are empty
  if (all(sapply(sets, length) == 0)) {
    return(
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = paste0(cc, "\n(no sig. DEPs)"),
                 size = 4) +
        theme_void() +
        labs(title = cc)
    )
  }

  ggVennDiagram(
    sets,
    label       = "count",
    label_alpha = 0,
    set_size    = 3.2
  ) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F5F5F5", high = "#4575B4") +
    labs(title = cc) +
    theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
})

names(plots_4way) <- contrasts

# ---- render 2x3 grid ----
wrap_plots(plots_4way, ncol = 3) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "DEP Overlap Across Covariate Models (FDR < 0.05)",
    subtitle = "Each region shows proteins significant in that model combination only",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40")
    )
  )
```

```{r}

library(vegan)
keep_m5   <- !is.na(meta_pca$ethnicity)
meta_m5p  <- meta_pca[keep_m5, ]
prot_m5p  <- prot_imp[keep_m5, , drop = FALSE]

meta_m5p


meta_m5p<- meta_m5p %>%
  dplyr::select(record_id, group, age, sex, bmi, hba1c, ethnicity) %>%
  drop_na()

prot_clean <- prot_m5p[meta_m5p$record_id, ]

adonis2(prot_clean ~ group + age + sex + bmi+ hba1c + ethnicity, data = meta_m5p)
```
```{r}
adonis2(
  prot_clean ~ group + age + sex + bmi + hba1c + ethnicity,
  data = meta_m5p,
  by = "margin",
  permutations = 9999
)
```
